validation_rules: If totals exist: total ~= subtotal + tax
If line items exist: total ~= sum(line_items.amount)
instructions:
Extraction Instructions:

File Name: Set to the input file name being processed.

hauler_name: Extract the company name at the top of the first page (e.g., “Republic Services”). Look for prominent branding or web/email addresses indicating the hauler.

account_number: Anchor on "Account Number", "Acct #", etc. Extract the value found directly below or to the right. Normalize by stripping spaces.

invoice_number: Anchor on "Invoice Number", "Invoice No", or "Invoice #". Take the adjacent or next line text; remove spaces.

invoice_date: Anchor on "Invoice Date" or similar. Extract nearby date; normalize to YYYY-MM-DD (e.g., "March 31, 2024" → "2024-03-31").

total_amount_due: Anchor on "Total Amount Due", “Amount Due”. Take adjacent or following number; remove $ and commas; convert to float.

payment_due_date: Anchor on "Payment Due Date", "Due Date". Take the adjacent or following value (may be a date or status like "Past Due").

service_address: Extract main address under or near company name or in line item block—look for patterns like “[Business Name] [Street Address], [City] [State] [ZIP]”. Choose the address associated with the relevant services, e.g., “Applebees 6315 S Interstate 35, Austin TX 78719”.

main_description: From line item/service section, extract the primary service description line, typically listing containers/types/lifts (e.g., “1 Waste Container 8 Cu Yd, 1 Lift Per Week”).

Normalization Rules:
- All numbers: remove $, commas.
- Dates: convert all to ISO format YYYY-MM-DD, using best guess if partial (default to 1st of month if only MM/YYYY).
- Strings: strip leading/trailing whitespace.

Line Item Extraction:

1. Identify the section between the “CURRENT INVOICE CHARGES” header and before any “Total” or summary lines. Anchor to tabular regions using headers: "Description", "Reference", "Quantity", "Unit Price", "Amount".

2. For each line:
    - quantity: Extract from the "Quantity" column (may be blank; if so, set to NaN).
    - unit_price: Extract from the "Unit Price" column; remove $, commas; convert to float (set NaN if missing).
    - line_total: Extract from the "Amount" or "Line Total" column; remove $, commas; convert to float (set NaN if missing).
    - File Name: Set to the input file name for each row.

3. Keep blank/NaN fields as needed to match input (e.g., for subtotal or header rows).

4. Maintain the order of line items as in the PDF.

General:
- If any field is missing, leave blank ("" for strings, NaN for numeric).
- Ignore repeated fields; use the first occurrence unless indicated otherwise by context.
- Do not extract handwritten or stamped values. Use only printed/OCR text.
